title: toxicodendron toxicarum f. elobatum
intro: Toxicodendron toxicarum f. elobatum, also known as the poison sumac, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of the plant are primarily derived from its sap, which contains urushiol, a toxic compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of using poison sumac include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. Herbal preparations of poison sumac include tinctures and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin issues, although it's essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic nature.
